title: gnaphalium subspicatum
intro: Gnaphalium subspicatum, also known as the New Zealand cudweed, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gnaphalium subspicatum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
